title: [Bug] Battery Drain while in sleep mode
author: kanjieater
body:
Describe the bug
Switch Lite runs hot and drains battery significantly faster than normal while in sleep mode with sys-DVR installed, even when not actively streaming. My lite also turns off the wifi when it goes into sleep. Sleep or no wifi should probably temporally slow the sysmodule down intentionally.

To Reproduce

Install sys-DVR on a Switch Lite
Put the console to sleep
Leave it for 30–60 minutes
Wake the console — it will be noticeably warm and battery will have drained faster than expected
Expected behavior
sys-DVR should not cause significant heat or battery drain while the console is in sleep mode and not actively streaming.

Setup information

PC Os: N/A
Console firmware: [21.1.0]
Custom firmware name and version: [e.g. Atmosphere 1.11.2]
SysDVR version: [6.3 Full]
Additional context
Issue appears to be caused by sys-DVR keeping CPU/network active during sleep. Removing sys-DVR from the sysmodule autostart resolves the heat and drain. Observed on Switch Lite hardware specifically; I did not see it on my V1 switch on 20.0.0 fw yet though, but i haven't been using it for very long. 

Is there a backoff that gets enabled while in sleep.

title: [Bug] ArgumentNullException when connecting to Nintendo Switch
author: Random-Penguin
body:
**Describe the bug**
```
Player connection failed: System.ArgumentNullException: Value cannot be null. (Parameter 'handle')
   at System.ArgumentNullException.Throw(String paramName)
   at System.Runtime.InteropServices.NativeLibrary.TryGetExport(IntPtr handle, String name, IntPtr& address)
   at FFmpeg.AutoGen.FunctionResolver.FindFunctionPointer(IntPtr nativeLibraryHandle, String functionName)
   at FFmpeg.AutoGen.FunctionResolver.GetFunctionDelegate[T](IntPtr nativeLibraryHandle, String functionName, Boolean throwOnError)
   at FFmpeg.AutoGen.FunctionResolver.GetFunctionDelegate[T](String libraryName, String functionName, Boolean throwOnError)
   at FFmpeg.AutoGen.DynamicallyLoadedBindings.<>c.<Initialize>b__2_354()
   at FFmpeg.AutoGen.ffmpeg.av_packet_alloc()
   at SysDVR.Client.Targets.Player.DecodeH264Target..ctor()
   at SysDVR.Client.Targets.Player.PlayerManager.MakeVideoStream(Boolean hasVideo)
   at SysDVR.Client.Targets.Player.PlayerManager..ctor(StreamingSource source, CancellationTokenSource cancel)
   at SysDVR.Client.Sources.DeviceConnector.ConnectForPlayer()
   at SysDVR.Client.GUI.ConnectingView.Created()
```

Happens after selecting my Switch from the list of consoles when trying to connect in USB or TCP modes.

**To Reproduce**
1. Open SysDVR
2. Connect to a Nintendo Switch in either Network or USB modes

**Expected behavior**
Not throw an exception and work correctly

**Setup information**
 - PC OS: Arch Linux rolling release, Hyprland 0.55.4
 - System firmware version: 20.5.0
 - Custom firmware name and version: Atmosphere 1.11.2
 - SysDVR version: SysDVR 6.3

**Additional context**
Running after building with:
```
dotnet restore
dotnet build -c Release
```

The software worked before today

title: [Bug] ImGui_ImplSDL2_InitForSDLRenderer not found
author: Random-Penguin
body:
**Describe the bug**
```
 git/sysdvr/Client/bin/Release/net9.0/SysDVR-Client
SysDVR-Client 6.3 - by exelix
https://github.com/exelix11/SysDVR
Build ID: 531e9c2

Initialized SDL with opengl renderer
System.EntryPointNotFoundException: Unable to find an entry point named 'ImGui_ImplSDL2_InitForSDLRenderer' in shared library 'cimgui'.
   at ImGuiNET.ImGuiSDL2Impl.InitForSDLRenderer(IntPtr window, IntPtr renderer)
   at SysDVR.Client.App.ClientApp.Entrypoint()
   at SysDVR.Client.Program.RunApp(String[] args)
```

**To Reproduce**
1. Build the client using the provided Makefile
2. Build the CimguiSDL2Cross shared library
3. Move cimgui.so to a directory recognised by the library loader or add the file directory to $LD_LIBRARY_PATH
4. Run the client

**Expected behavior**
I would have imagined it to run.

**Setup information**
 - PC OS: Arch Linux rolling release, using Hyprland 0.55.4
 - Console firmware: N/A
 - Custom firmware name and version: N/A
 - SysDVR version: 6.3

**Additional context**
I can provide additional information if asked.

title: [Help] 10057 error
author: KantoBuxa
body:
**Where do you get stuck ?**
I start the stream on my Nintendo Switch, but the Switch does not appear on my PC for connection. I tried manual connection, but I get an error code. Finally, I also tried using USB, but the Nintendo Switch does not appear there either. Both the SysDVR version on the Switch and on the PC are the same and fully up to date.

**What's the error message you get ?**
SysDVR-Client 6.3 - by exelix
https://github.com/exelix11/SysDVR

at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.AwaitableSocketAsyncEventArgs.CreateException(SocketError error, Boolean forAsyncThrow)

* SysDVR version: SysDVR-Client-Windows-x64-com-framework

**Additional context**
Both SysDVR versions on the Nintendo Switch and PC are fully updated and match each other.

title: [Bug] Stream freezes when IR camera is fully active
author: VilcsiTH
body:
**Describe the bug**
When the IR camera is fully activated, stream stops with the same header error that would come up when system menus are present.

**To Reproduce**
Using WarioWare: Move It, try playing the Hand Model minigames "Rock paper scissors", or "Safe snacker".

**Expected behavior**
Stream isn't interrupted

**Setup information**
 - PC Os: Win11
 - Console firmware 20.5.0
 - Custom firmware name and version Atmosphere 1.11.1
 - SysDVR version 6.3 USB

**Additional context**
The IR camera has two modes according to PC Gaming Wiki: Far/Narrow 75° and Near/Wide 130°. WarioWare: Move It has a few IR camera minigames, and on the bottom of the JoyCon you can see the difference between the two modes: Either 2 lights show up, or 4. I assume Narrow mode has 2 lights, and this one works perfectly with SysDVR, while Wide mode has 4 lights, and freezes the stream.

title: [Feature request] Homebrew tap for macOS users?
author: lexicalnerd
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Not really a problem but currently, installation requires manually downloading and managing SysDVR. This makes version tracking, updates, and system integration less convenient for users who rely on package managers.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Creating a Homebrew tap for those who use the `brew` package manager would be great. Creating a tap is easy ([see here](https://docs.brew.sh/How-to-Create-and-Maintain-a-Tap)). The current solution works, but a Homebrew tap (and possibly one that got published into the mainstream) would be beneficial for those who want a straightforward installation.

**Additional context**
Check out example SysDVR tap implementation here: [lexicalnerd/tap](https://github.com/lexicalnerd/homebrew-tap/blob/main/Formula/sysdvr.rb).

title: [Not an Issue] Compatibility with DefenderOfHyrule idn_mitm fork
author: GioroGiori
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
After the 20.0.X firmware update, ldn_mitm and SysDVR don't get along, causing atmosphere crash, before firmware 20 they don't had any issues and can coexist each other, so games like Pokemon Sword can't be played in SysDVR bc of the LAN mode, and to use idn_mitm is necesary to disable SysDVR, making it useless to play games who use LAN mode or the DVR patches

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Compatibility with the defender of hyrule fork ([ldn_mitm](https://github.com/DefenderOfHyrule/ldn_mitm)) 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Uninstalling idn_mitm to avoid crashes and use SysDVR in games compatibles

**Additional context**
I dunno if this is a idn_mitm issue or a SysDVR issue, but simply put is necesary to uninstall idn_mitm to use it so I make a feature request, if this is the wrong place to ask, you can close this
